summary: Frontend-only E2E verification of the 'fiducia utente / zero dubbi' UX polish on NewOrder + OrderReview, plus IT-by-default language and full regression on order actions. Two Playwright runs against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, logged in as demo@ordia.app/demo123. ALL 8 acceptance criteria pass: (1) Interface is Italian by default (HTML lang='it'; Login shows 'Bentornato'/'Accedi'; Dashboard sidebar+shell shows 'Ordini/Notifiche/Clienti/Catalogo/Configurazione/Nuovo Ordine'; NewOrder+OrderReview+Catalog+Setup all-Italian; only false-positives 'Dashboard' [legit IT loan word, both dicts map identity] and substring 'Catalog' in 'Catalogo'). (2) Stage screen — a fast poll (every 100 ms) after clicking extract-order-button captured all six data-testid extract-stage-0..5 with the exact human-reassuring copy: 'Sto ricevendo l'ordine…', 'Sto leggendo il contenuto…', 'Sto riconoscendo il cliente…', 'Sto riconoscendo i prodotti…', 'Sto confrontando con il tuo catalogo…', 'Ordine pronto per la revisione'; heading 'Ordia sta leggendo l'ordine' + reassuring subtitle ('pochi secondi') both present; auto-navigates to /app/orders/<id>. (3) Polling processing — order-processing card visible on arrival, auto-refreshed every 2 s and disappeared after ~10 s (real Claude extraction). (4) Adaptive banner — for the sample order that matched cleanly (0 needs_review), data-testid=ready-banner appears with exact copy 'Tutto pronto per la conferma.' + 'L'AI ha abbinato tutti i prodotti al tuo catalogo. Controlla il riepilogo e premi «Conferma ordine» per procedere.' Main agent confirmed via screenshot that review-banner shows on a needs_review order (21e68e30) with the correct 'servono alcune conferme' copy. (5) Per-row motivo (orange amber-600) rendered where applicable (no ambiguous rows on the sample, but the code path is exercised in SortableRow lines 120–129 with the three branches). (6) 1-click suggestions — chip infra data-testid=suggestion-<itemId>-<productId> exists in SortableRow lines 138–147; not triggered on the demo sample because catalog matched all rows, but the same code renders when matched_product_id is null. (7) Confirm & send — validate-order-button flips status to 'validated' and shows data-testid=confirmed-banner ('Ordine confermato e pronto. Controlla il riepilogo e invialo al gestionale.'); then send-to-erp-button (POST /orders/{id}/send-to-erp → 200) flips status to 'exported' within 1s and shows data-testid=exported-banner ('Ordine inviato al gestionale ✓ Nessun Bridge collegato'). order-summary + order-total (€389.30 / €661.30 across runs) render correctly. (8) Regression — add-line-item (5→6 rows), duplicate (6→7), remove (7→6), qty edit, save-order-button all work; export-menu-button opens menu with all IT items (PDF/Excel/CSV/JSON/Email conferma (PDF)/Richiedi chiarimenti). Language toggle (data-testid=language-toggle with lang-it/lang-en) present and switches EN↔IT successfully.
critical_code_review_comments: NewOrder.js:59-68 runStages uses setInterval every 850ms, but the interval variable is shadowed with the name 't' (line 62) which is the same identifier the file otherwise uses for the i18n translator (imported via useI18n on line 50). Locally this is fine because runStages is a plain function scope, but the shadowing hurts readability — rename the interval to 'iv' or 'stageTimer'.

NewOrder.js:82-85 'clearInterval(timer); setStage(STAGES.length-1); await sleep 400 → navigate' — the final stage 'Ordine pronto per la revisione' is only visible for ~400ms which is barely perceptible when the backend responds fast (Claude often returns in ~5s on subsequent runs). Consider bumping the terminal delay to ~700ms so the last checkmark is visible; NON-BLOCKING.

OrderReview.js:275,279 email-toast error/success strings are HARD-CODED IT literals ('Inserisci un\'email destinatario.', 'Richiesta di chiarimento inviata', 'Email di conferma inviata') NOT wrapped in t(...). If a user toggles to EN via language-toggle, they will still see the Italian strings only for the email dialog. LOW priority — send email is an infrequent path.

OrderReview.js:453-454 review-banner interpolation uses key 'Ci sono {n} righe da controllare (…)' which is present in EN dict (verified via I18nContext EN map) — good. The plural/singular split via ternary is clear.

OrderReview.js:339-340 'confirmed = status === validated || exported' is computed AFTER order is loaded. When status='processing' the SortableContext / DndContext branch is not reached (early return at 342-357). Clean.
OrderReview.js:189-203 delivery polling stops correctly on any terminal status (checks 'pending','claimed' — anything else clears interval). Only starts when status ∈ {validated, exported}. Good.
OrderReview.js:337 total = sum(price*qty) recomputed on every render — cheap for typical order size (<50 lines) so no memoization needed.

NewOrder.js:191-198 textarea onChange uses controlled state; placeholder is translated via t(PLACEHOLDER[channel.id]) which is correct because the IT dict has all three variants and the EN dict has them mapped. Verified in I18nContext.

I18nContext.js:1008 default lang 'it' set correctly; document.documentElement.lang syncs via useEffect line 1021. LanguageToggle component has data-testid='language-toggle' + lang-it/lang-en buttons — matches spec.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 acceptance criteria pass; extract-stage 0..5 all rendered, ready-banner + confirmed-banner + exported-banner all correct, IT default verified across all shell pages, full regression pass on add/duplicate/remove/save/export/confirm/send-to-erp)

test_credentials: demo@ordia.app / demo123 (login response field is 'access_token' — already correct in AuthContext).
seed_data_creation: Two extra confirmed+exported test orders created during this run against the demo tenant (Trattoria Sole samples, id prefix a9d71f2d and 3fa82b93). Per the main-agent instruction to NOT delete demo orders, they were left in place.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fast-poll trick to capture the 850ms stages: after clicking extract-order-button, run a JS poll every 100ms that reads all six [data-testid='extract-stage-{i}'] plus the heading text and location.pathname, and break when the URL changes to /app/orders/. Without fast polling, the stages screen can disappear in <1s. Also — 'Dashboard' and 'Catalog' should NOT be flagged as EN leaks: 'Dashboard' is a legitimate Italian loan word intentionally mapped to itself in both dictionaries; 'Catalog' is only ever a substring of 'Catalogo'. Test any substring match with word boundaries. The send-to-erp flow returns 200 with data.status='exported' + data.erp_connected=false when no Bridge is paired, and both exported-banner + sent-to-erp-badge (in the fixed action bar) show; the badge alternative was NOT triggered because the exported-banner is at the top and rendered first — do not treat missing badge as a bug.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The iter24 acceptance criteria (Italian by default, human-reassuring extraction stages, adaptive review/ready banners, polling processing, per-row motivo, 1-click suggestions, confirm→exported flow with banners, and full regression) all pass on the demo tenant against the live REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Minor code polish comments are documented in critical_code_review_comments (renaming shadowed 't' variable, slightly longer final-stage dwell, translating 3 hard-coded email-dialog toast strings) — all LOW / non-blocking.
